body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Define a proper type for transaction input to fix pipeline failure.**

The `any` type is causing the ESLint/TypeScript pipeline to fail. Define an interface for the expected transaction structure.


<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
+interface TransactionInput {
+  amount: string | number;
+  category?: string;
+  type?: string;
+  note?: string;
+  date?: string;
+}

// In the map function:
-    const formattedTransactions = transactions.map((t: any) => ({
+    const formattedTransactions = transactions.map((t: TransactionInput) => ({
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🧰 Tools</summary>

<details>
<summary>🪛 GitHub Actions: CI & Code Quality</summary>

[error] 21-21: ESLint (`@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`): Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

</details>
<details>
<summary>🪛 GitHub Check: lint-and-typecheck</summary>

[failure] 21-21: 
Unexpected any. Specify a different type

</details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts` at line 21, Replace the usage of the
`any` type in the transactions mapping by declaring a proper interface (e.g.,
TransactionInput) that captures the expected fields used inside the map, then
update the mapping signature from `transactions.map((t: any) => ...)` to
`transactions.map((t: TransactionInput) => ...)`; locate the mapping that
produces `formattedTransactions` and ensure the new interface is
exported/defined near the top of the file (or imported) so TypeScript/ESLint
accept the concrete type for `transactions` and eliminate the pipeline failure.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:ocelot -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**`parseFloat` can produce `NaN`, which will be stored in the database.**

If `t.amount` is not a valid numeric string (e.g., `"abc"` or `undefined`), `parseFloat` returns `NaN`. Prisma will attempt to insert this, potentially causing database errors or storing invalid data.


<details>
<summary>🛡️ Proposed fix with validation</summary>

```diff
-    const formattedTransactions = transactions.map((t: any) => ({
-      amount: parseFloat(t.amount),
+    const formattedTransactions = transactions
+      .map((t: TransactionInput) => {
+        const amount = parseFloat(String(t.amount));
+        if (isNaN(amount)) return null;
+        return {
+          amount,
           category: t.category || "Uncategorized",
-      type: t.type || "EXPENSE",
+          type: ["INCOME", "EXPENSE"].includes(t.type?.toUpperCase() ?? "") 
+            ? t.type!.toUpperCase() 
+            : "EXPENSE",
           note: t.note || "",
           date: t.date ? new Date(t.date) : new Date(),
           userId: session.user.id,
-    }))
+        };
+      })
+      .filter((t): t is NonNullable<typeof t> => t !== null);
+
+    if (formattedTransactions.length === 0) {
+      return NextResponse.json({ error: "No valid transactions after parsing" }, { status: 400 });
+    }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts` at line 22, The mapping that builds
transactions uses parseFloat on t.amount and can produce NaN (see the amount:
parseFloat(t.amount) in src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts); validate the
parsed value before saving by replacing the direct parseFloat call with a check:
parse the value, verify Number.isFinite(parsedAmount) (or
!Number.isNaN(parsedAmount)), and if invalid return a 4xx validation error (or
skip/normalize according to API contract) instead of inserting NaN into the
Prisma create; adjust the code around the transaction-mapping/creation logic
(the function that constructs the bulk transactions array) to enforce this
validation and include a clear error message referencing the offending input.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:ocelot -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

+import { NextResponse } from "next/server"
+import { getServerSession } from "next-auth/next"
+import { authOptions } from "@/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route"
+import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"
+
+export async function POST(req: Request) {
+  const session = await getServerSession(authOptions)
+
+  if (!session || !session.user || !session.user.id) {
+    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 })
+  }
+
+  try {
+    const { transactions } = await req.json()
+
+    if (!transactions || !Array.isArray(transactions) || transactions.length === 0) {
+      return NextResponse.json({ error: "No transactions provided" }, { status: 400 })
+    }
+
+    // Format data for Prisma createMany
+    const formattedTransactions = transactions.map((t: any) => ({
+      amount: parseFloat(t.amount),
+      category: t.category || "Uncategorized",
+      type: t.type || "EXPENSE",
body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Type field accepts arbitrary strings without validation.**

The `type` field accepts any string value, but the domain model expects "INCOME" or "EXPENSE". Invalid types like "DEBIT" or typos will be silently accepted, potentially breaking filtering and calculations elsewhere in the app.



Consider normalizing and validating the type value, or at minimum, converting to uppercase for consistency:

```diff
-      type: t.type || "EXPENSE",
+      type: (t.type?.toUpperCase() === "INCOME" ? "INCOME" : "EXPENSE"),
```

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
      type: (t.type?.toUpperCase() === "INCOME" ? "INCOME" : "EXPENSE"),
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/app/api/transactions/bulk/route.ts` at line 24, The transaction `type` is
currently set with `type: t.type || "EXPENSE"` which allows arbitrary strings;
update this by normalizing and validating `t.type` (e.g., convert to uppercase)
and only accept "INCOME" or "EXPENSE"—if the normalized value is neither, fall
back to "EXPENSE". Implement this check where `t.type` is used (the mapping that
sets `type`) or extract it to a helper like `normalizeTransactionType(type)` and
replace `type: t.type || "EXPENSE"` with the validated/normalized result to
ensure downstream code only sees "INCOME" or "EXPENSE".
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:ocelot -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Filtering by truthy `amount` excludes legitimate zero-value transactions.**

The filter `.filter(t => t.amount)` uses a truthy check, which will exclude rows where amount is `0`, `"0"`, or `""`. While `""` should be filtered, a zero amount might be a valid transaction (e.g., a $0 promotional credit).

Consider explicitly checking for the presence and validity of the amount instead.


<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-          }).filter(t => t.amount); // Filter rows without amounts
+          }).filter(t => t.amount !== undefined && t.amount !== null && t.amount !== "");
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
          }).filter(t => t.amount !== undefined && t.amount !== null && t.amount !== "");
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/components/Dashboard.tsx` at line 226, The current filter `.filter(t =>
t.amount)` in Dashboard.tsx erroneously drops valid zero amounts; replace it
with an explicit presence/validity check on t.amount (e.g., ensure t.amount is
not null/undefined and not the empty string) so that numeric 0 or "0" are
preserved while still excluding blank values; update the filter expression where
rows are filtered (referencing the `.filter(t => t.amount)` call and the
t.amount field) and, if downstream code assumes numeric values, coerce or
validate the amount to a Number after the filter.
```

</details>
